<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot -->
# Accessibility Checklist

This checklist standardizes accessibility validation for shell and remote micro-app changes.

Target standard: WCAG 2.2 AA.

---

## Required Checks Before PR

1. Keyboard-only navigation covers shell header, side navigation, and at least one route in each changed app.
2. All interactive controls are reachable with Tab and operable with Enter or Space.
3. Focus indicator is visible and not hidden by custom styling.
4. Landmark structure is preserved (`header`, `nav`, `main`, and dialog roles as needed).
5. Dynamic status content uses `role="status"` or `aria-live` where appropriate.
6. Error and warning content uses `role="alert"` when user action is required.
7. Icon-only controls include accessible labels.
8. Color is not the only channel for critical state.
9. Chart or visual-only sections have textual alternatives.
10. New images include `alt` text, or empty alt text for decorative content.

---

## Automated Evidence

| Area | Evidence Source | Command |
|------|-----------------|---------|
| Shell navigation accessibility | `apps/shell/src/components/SideNav.test.tsx` jest-axe checks | `npm test` |
| Shared UI component accessibility | Existing component tests in `libs/shared/ui-components/src/lib/*.test.tsx` | `npm test` |
| Integration-level regressions | Root test suite in CI | `npm test` |

---

## Keyboard Validation Script

Run this manual flow in local development for every PR that affects shell navigation or layout.

1. Open shell at `http://localhost:4200`.
2. Use Tab from the browser URL bar into app content.
3. Confirm collapse and expand navigation controls are focusable and operable.
4. Navigate to Agentic Chat and back using keyboard only.
5. Open the user menu, open profile modal, and close it without mouse input.
6. Trigger one remote route transition from navigation and verify focus remains visible.
7. Verify no keyboard trap exists in modal or side navigation.

---

## CI Gate Recommendation

CI should fail when accessibility checks regress in changed projects.

1. Keep jest-axe checks in smoke-level tests for shell and shared components.
2. Add targeted a11y assertions whenever new interactive patterns are introduced.
3. Treat accessibility regressions as release-blocking defects.
